§6MOX Fuel§f

§aMixed oxide fuel — mid-range nuclear fuel that blends uranium and plutonium!§f

§bMOX Fuel§f (Mixed Oxide Fuel) is a mixture of §e2/3rds§f uranium and §e1/3rd§f plutonium fuel. In most reactors it's mid-range, but in the §bRBMK§f it's considerably strong for its cost.

§6Composition§f:
§b• §e2/3§f Medium Enriched U-235 Fuel
§b• §e1/3§f Pu-239

§6Crafting§f (shapeless):
§b• Nugget§f: 4 Uranium Fuel Nuggets + 2 Pu-239 Nuggets → MOX Fuel Billet
§b• Billet§f: 2 Uranium Fuel Billets + 1 Pu-239 Billet → §e3§f MOX Fuel Billets

§6ZIRNOX Performance§f:
§b• Heat§f: §e75§f/tick (stronger than plutonium)
§b• Lifespan§f: §e165k§f ticks (somewhat low)
§b• Radiation§f: §e2.5§f RAD/s start → §e150§f RAD/s end

§6RBMK Performance§f:
§b• Yield§f: §e1B§f
§b• Function§f: Logarithmic
§b• Heat per flux§f: §e7.5§f TU

§6Recycling§f (PUREX):
§b• Depleted MOX§f → Reactor Grade Pu Nugget + Tc-99 Nugget + 3 Nuclear Waste

§eTip§f: MOX fuel offers great value for the RBMK. The billet recipe gives §e3§f billets instead of 1 — extremely efficient! Use your Pu-239 and Uranium Fuel surplus for bulk MOX production.
